programmes.show_description: Sir Tom Jones: In conversation and song, Sir Tom Jones shares captivating stories from his career - including his friendship with Elvis Presley. (S1, ep 1)

programmes.show_name: Simon & Garfunkel: Old Friends Live on Stage
programmes.show_description: The Duo perform their third live album with performances taken from shows at Madison Square Garden and New Jersey in December 2003.
Ehrenbreitstein, one of the finest paintings by JMW Turner still in private hands, is up for auction at Sotheby's in London where it is expected to fetch around £20million. (S10, ep 6)
Sotheby's auctions a range of paintings from the 20th and 21st centuries which are no bigger than the size of the catalogue page on which it is illustrated, in actual size. (S10, ep 7)
The theatre with a revolving auditorium in Cesk? Krumlov, Czech Republic, is where Messiah comes alive in a magical atmosphere of light and dark.
Galloping Foxley: John Mills stars in this twisted tale. A man starts to suffer when he becomes haunted by the memory of the brutal treatment he received in his childhood. (S2, ep 2)
Georgy Porgy: Joan Collins stars in a twisted tale. George has problems with female attention, but when a beautiful woman joins his congregation things are set to change. (S2, ep 3)
Royal Jelly: Herbert Wise stars in this twisted tale. Mabel's baby is sick and her bee-obsessed husband thinks he has found a remedy. Will he get the baby buzzing back to health? (S2, ep 4)
Poison: Andrew Ray stars in Roald Dahl's twisted tale. A poisonous snake keeps a teacher prisoner in India. How can he escape from its evil glare? (S2, ep 5)
The Hitch-Hiker: Rod Taylor stars in Roald Dahl's twisted tale. When a driver picks up a hitch-hiker in his car, his journey turns into a thrilling ride. (S2, ep 6)
A Matter of Murder: A notorious auto thief and his gang steal a Rolls-Royce, unaware that the trunk contains the body of a woman who was murdered by her husband. (S2, ep 23)
The Gentleman Caller: When a man and his wife attempt to do away with an elderly lady, they discover that she is not as senile as they had imagined. (S2, ep 24)
Audrey Hepburn stars as a bored and sheltered princess on the escape from her guardians, subsequently falling in love with an American newsman in Rome. Romance. (1953)
Film critics Ian Nathan, Bonnie Greer, Neil Norman and Stephen Armstrong discuss their favourite romance movies of all time, including Casablanca and When Harry Met Sally.
Join the pioneering country music supergroup of Willie Nelson, Waylon Jennings, Johnny Cash and Kris Kristofferson for their epic 1990 concert from the Nassau Coliseum.

programmes.show_name: Johnny Cash: A Legend in Concert
programmes.show_description: Fantastic collection of recordings from Johnny Cash's early years, including nostalgic performances of I Got Stripes, Don't Take Your Guns To Town and Five Feet High and Rising.
The late Johnny Cash brought his inimitable sound to the Montreux Festival in 1994, featuring favourites from across his career including Folsom Prison Blues and more.

programmes.show_name: FILM: For A Few Dollars More
programmes.show_description: Two bounty killers with similar intentions but different motivations team up to take down a psychotic Mexican outlaw before he and his gang can carry out an audacious bank robbery. (1965)
...Margins To The Mainstream. This documentary explores how from 1970-1977, six low-budget films shown at midnight transformed the way we make and watch films.

programmes.show_name: Dexys Midnight Runners: Greatest Albums Live
programmes.show_description: Dexys Midnight Runners: The iconic rock band perform and discuss Too-Rye-Ay, their beloved 1982 album, featuring the hit single Come On Eileen. (S1, ep 1)

programmes.show_name: Pretenders: Live And Loose In L.A.
programmes.show_description: The Pretenders' sold-out gig at LA's Wiltern Theatre, where Chrissie Hynde and the band performed all their classic hits, from Brass in Pocket to Back On the Chain Gang.
The story behind the writing, recording and success of Black Sabbath's second album, Paranoid, one of the most influential albums of the heavy metal genre.
John Neumeier's A Midsummer Night's Dream weaves tangled love, jealousy and magic as fairies, mortals and rustics collide in a dreamlike world.
Genesis and Catastrophe: A Roald Dahl story with a twist. A pregnant lady is worried that she may miscarry for the fourth time. Can her doctor reassure her? (S2, ep 7)
Mr Botibol's First Love: Jake Weston stars in this twisted story. A jaded businessman sells his company and attempts to change his life for the better by buying a strange piano. (S2, ep 8)
The Umbrella Man: John Mills stars in this story with a twist. A con man arrives in town and starts his scam. Michael Gambon guest stars. (S2, ep 9)
The Flypaper: Lorna Charles and Alfred Burke star in this twisted tale. With a 12 year-old found dead and a killer perhaps on the loose, a young girl becomes paranoid. (S2, ep 10)
The Orderly World of Mr Appleby: Robert Lang stars in this tale with a twist. A man married three times might be lucky for the fourth time when he meets a new interest. (S2, ep 11)
The Ordeal Of Mrs. Snow: An elderly woman is locked in an air tight safe with one of her cats by her niece's husband when she discovers he is a forger. (S2, ep 25)
Ten Minutes From Now: An unsuccessful artist accused of bomb threats devises a scheme to steal artworks. (S2, ep 26)
The Scream: More than a painting, The Scream provides a glimpse into Edvard Munch's mind. Learn his struggles with anxiety and the deterioration of his works over time. (S1, ep 1)
Starry Night: Trace the tragedy of Van Gogh's short life, the brilliance born from pain and the legacy of a genius whose masterpiece continues to captivate and inspire. (S1, ep 2)
Elaine Paige stars in Andrew Lloyd Webber's world-famous musical. A group of cats gather to choose one lucky feline, who will go to a magical place to be reborn.
In Oklahoma territory at the turn of the 20th century, two young cowboys vie with an evil ranch hand and a traveling peddler for the hearts of the women they love.
Iconic Women Of Country opens up the music vaults to provide an intimate look at legendary female artists, their stories and songs and why they've stood the test of time.
Audrey Hepburn stars as a bored and sheltered princess on the escape from her guardians, subsequently falling in love with an American newsman in Rome. Romance. (1953)

programmes.show_name: Elvis Presley: A Legend in Concert
programmes.show_description: Sensational collection of TV performances from The King. Featuring the notorious screen debut of his famous pelvic gyrations and the time Steve Allen had him sing to a basset hound.

programmes.show_name: Sammy Davis Jr: Legends In Concert
programmes.show_description: A collection of the original Rat-Packer's most memorable stage appearances, including performances of The Birth of the Blues and You Rascal You.
